title: Carissa comorensis

side_effects: Carissa comorensis is known to be toxic and its ingestion can cause symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. intro: Carissa comorensis, also known as the Natal plum or Bird of Paradise, is a deciduous shrub native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive disorders, as well as its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Carissa comorensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Carissa comorensis is often made into a tea, juice, or decoction, with the most common preparations including a fruit juice or a decoction of the leaves and fruits.
